title: Cancer Diagnosis Pivot
theme: A town never forgets. It just redecorates.
omen: a child saying mommy was loud last night when she was alone
relationship_dynamic: disbelieving best friend learns too much

starter_premise.title: Cancer Diagnosis Pivot
starter_premise.premise: A diagnosis reshapes everything, estranged people reconcile, and the crying is beautiful.
starter_premise.signature_tell: The diagnosis always happens in minute twelve before she has done anything fun.

cast.name: Jenna Mercer
traits: seems one kitchen confession away from headline syntax
says he would not stop in a way nobody enjoys hearing twice
smiles like a restraining order is considering her
secret_capacity: deleted 37 messages and remembers every word of them

cast.space_description: unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files
cast.suspicion_profile: knows where a secret could fit architecturally

has a second phone labeled as a work precaution

cast.space_description: old storm shelter from before the divorce

beat_sheet.text: The wound is present from scene one: A diagnosis reshapes everything, estranged people reconcile, and the crying is beautiful. Jenna Mercer enters Marigold Falls or stays trapped inside it while trying to contain the damage through competence, routine, or denial. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Luke Sutton hesitates before explaining old storm shelter from before the divorce. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Luke Sutton is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Jenna Mercer says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Blackthorn Lake.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Luke?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Rachel. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Jenna. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The nanny cam memory card is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Claire."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
"Honey, ever since the accident, your anxiety has been so high. Did you take your pills?"
"That pool was supposed to make us look safe, and somehow it became a witness."
"He keeps calling it stress like stress knows my garage code."
"If she is the nanny, why is she wearing my robe like a legal argument?"
fake_critic_blurb: "Cancer Diagnosis Pivot understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "Cancer Diagnosis Pivot" follows Jenna Mercer. Jenna Mercer is a possible victim or possible defendant. A diagnosis reshapes everything, estranged people reconcile, and the crying is beautiful. She had always known. Luke Sutton enters the picture carrying calm, plausible access, and the kind of concern that becomes heavier each time it is repeated. The signature tell is always this: The diagnosis always happens in minute twelve before she has done anything fun. A child saying mommy was loud last night when she was alone becomes part of the record. The weight is in the facts, and the facts do not improve when spoken aloud. Where every secret comes home.
